engineGuide.h2: Range Rover Velar Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Range Rover Velar across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0 Litre Diesel
entries.history: The higher-output twin-turbo Ingenium diesel, powering D240 and later D250 Velar variants. Higher cylinder pressures create greater failure risk than the single-turbo unit.
compatibleVariants: D180, D180 SE, D180 R-Dynamic, D180 S
D240, D240 SE, D240 R-Dynamic, D240 HSE, D250, D250 R-Dynamic
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch - primary failure mode, typically 50,000-80,000 miles
Oil dilution from incomplete DPF regeneration cycles
Turbo oil feed pipe leak at filter assembly connection
Twin-turbo failure sending metal fragments through engine
Oil dilution leading to bottom-end bearing failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for 204DTA Range Rover Velar engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: JLR’s Ingenium petrol four-cylinder. Generally more reliable than its diesel counterpart, but affected by a major cooling system fire risk recall affecting 2017-2023 models.
compatibleVariants: P250, P250 S, P250 SE, P250 R-Dynamic, P300, P300 R-Dynamic
commonFailures: Cooling system failure - engine fire risk recall (2017-2023)
Coolant leak from cooling system components
Timing chain tensioner wear (lower incidence than diesel)
entries.cta: Get quotes for PT204 Range Rover Velar engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0 Litre Supercharged Petrol
entries.history: Ford-derived supercharged V6 shared with Jaguar F-Pace and XF. Delivers the classic supercharged V6 character before the Velar range consolidated around Ingenium four-cylinders.
compatibleVariants: P340, P340 R-Dynamic, P340 HSE, P380, P380 R-Dynamic, P380 HSE, First Edition
commonFailures: Supercharger bearing wear and coupler failure
Coolant leaks from plastic crossover pipes
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ126 Range Rover Velar engine replacement

entries.title: 5.0 Litre Supercharged Petrol (Limited)
entries.history: The legendary Ford-derived 5.0 V8, reserved for top-tier Velar models. Not a factory UK option - V8 Velars are typically aftermarket conversions or grey imports. Extremely rare.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ133 Range Rover Velar engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0 Litre Petrol Hybrid (P400e)
entries.history: The plug-in hybrid variant of the Velar, combining the PT204 petrol engine with an electric motor and battery pack. Complex hybrid system requires specialist replacement expertise.
entries.cta: Get quotes for PT400e Range Rover Velar hybrid engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Range Rover Velar Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Range Rover Velar’s 2.0-litre Ingenium diesel engines (D180, D240, D250) dominate UK replacement enquiries, with timing chain stretch and oil dilution failures accounting for over 80% of catastrophic engine seizures. Petrol variants (P250, P300) suffer fewer failures but carry a significant cooling system fire risk recall affecting 2017-2023 models. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded timing chain components and improved piston ring designs specifically engineered to address the original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Below, we break down the four most common Velar engine failures, when repair is viable, and when full replacement becomes the only cost-effective solution.

problems.group: Range Rover Velar Ingenium Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Range Rover Velar Ingenium Timing Chain Failure - Why 2.0 Diesel Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Range Rover Velar D180, D200, D240, D250 (2017-2024, engine codes 204DTD single-turbo, 204DTA twin-turbo, AJ20D4)
problems.rootCause: Underspecified timing chain tensioner and guide rails combined with extended oil change intervals. The chain stretches progressively, eventually skipping teeth on the camshaft sprocket or snapping completely, causing piston-to-valve contact.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Chain Replacement - Catch it early (rattle on cold start, no fault codes)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain kit, tensioners, guides, and oil pump chain
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught before chain jumps teeth. Adds 40,000-60,000 miles

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Chain Replacement + Cylinder Head Repair - After skipped timing (compression loss, valve damage)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Recondition cylinder head, replace bent valves, full timing kit replacement
repairOptions.longevity: For engines with minor valve contact but intact block. 40,000-60,000 mile lifespan

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Chain snapped or seized (crank no start, catastrophic failure)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit reconditioned long engine with upgraded timing components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: The only realistic option after catastrophic failure. Factory-spec or better reliability

problems.recommendation: On a Velar worth £15,000-£25,000, preventative chain replacement at the first sign of cold-start rattle offers exceptional value - £1,500-£2,400 at a specialist versus £8,500+ for a replacement engine. However, many owners discover the failure only after the engine seizes. If your Velar has already suffered a snapped chain or crank-no-start condition, skip the repair attempts and go straight to a reconditioned long engine with upgraded components.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 204DTA and 204DTD engine prices for your Range Rover Velar

problems.group: Range Rover Velar Oil Dilution Failure
problems.h4: Range Rover Velar Oil Dilution Failure - The Silent Ingestion Killer
problems.affectedModels: Range Rover Velar D180, D240 (2017-2022, 204DTD, 204DTA), higher incidence in vehicles used primarily for short journeys
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 40,000 - 70,000 miles (directly linked to driving pattern, not age)
problems.rootCause: Incomplete DPF regeneration cycles dump diesel fuel past piston rings into the engine oil. Rising oil level reduces viscosity until bearings fail catastrophically - often without prior warning lights.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Oil Dilution Reversal & DPF Remediation - Caught early (oil level high, diesel smell, no knocking)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Oil and filter change with correct STJLR.03.5006 spec, forced DPF regen, injector leak-off test, ECU software update
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught before bearing damage occurs. No change to underlying hardware weakness

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Bottom end knock, seized engine (oil turned to solvent, bearings spun)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit reconditioned long engine with upgraded piston rings, revised injector seals, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Only realistic option once bearing failure occurs. Upgraded rings significantly reduce recurrence risk

problems.recommendation: Check your oil level weekly. If it rises between services or smells strongly of diesel, act immediately. A £450-£800 specialist intervention can save your engine. Once you hear bottom-end knock or the engine seizes, the block is scrap - replacement is your only path forward. For high-mileage or frequent short-trip Velars, consider switching to 8,000-mile oil change intervals regardless of the manufacturer schedule.
problems.cta: Get quotes for oil dilution-resistant reconditioned Range Rover Velar engines

problems.h4: Range Rover Velar Twin-Turbo Failure - When Boost Sends Metal Through Your Engine
problems.affectedModels: Range Rover Velar D240, D250 (2017-2024, 204DTA twin-turbo variant only - D180 single-turbo not affected)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000 - 100,000 miles (higher cylinder pressures than single-turbo accelerate wear)
problems.rootCause: The twin-turbo arrangement on the 204DTA engine subjects the smaller high-pressure turbo to extreme thermal cycling. Bearing failure sends metal fragments through the intake system directly into combustion chambers, scoring cylinder walls and destroying piston rings.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Turbo Replacement Only - Caught immediately (smoke, loss of power, no engine knock yet)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace both turbo units, flush oil and intake system, inspect bores with borescope
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught before metal reaches cylinders. Turbo failure symptoms must be acted on immediately

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Metal contamination confirmed (cylinder scoring, knocking, oil full of debris)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit reconditioned long engine with new turbo units, upgraded bearings, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Mandatory if cylinders are scored. Cannot reliably repair - debris remains embedded in block

problems.recommendation: If your D240 or D250 loses power or smokes, stop driving immediately. A £2,200-£3,500 specialist turbo replacement can save the engine if no metal has entered the cylinders. Continue driving and the engine will self-destruct. Once you hear knocking or a borescope reveals scoring, the block is compromised - replacement engines are the only safe option.
problems.cta: Compare 204DTA twin-turbo Velar engine replacement prices from UK specialists

problems.group: Range Rover Velar Cooling System Fire Risk
problems.h4: Range Rover Velar Cooling System Fire Risk - The PT204 Petrol Recall Failure
problems.affectedModels: Range Rover Velar P250, P300 (2017-2023, engine code PT204 Ingenium petrol)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Unpredictable - recall affects all 2017-2023 production regardless of mileage
problems.rootCause: Coolant system component failure causes rapid overheating and pressurisation, leading to coolant ejection and engine fire risk. JLR issued a formal recall covering 2017-2023 models after multiple documented fires.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Cooling System Overhaul - No symptoms, proactive replacement before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace coolant pump, expansion tank, hoses, and update ECU software with revised calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents the fire risk entirely. One-time preventative cost

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Post-Failure Engine Repair - Overheated but not seized (warped head, blown gasket)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head skim, gasket replacement, cooling system overhaul, oil and coolant flush
repairOptions.longevity: 40,000-60,000 mile lifespan after repair. Risk of hidden damage remains

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Seized from overheating (crank no start, coolant in oil, fire damage)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit reconditioned short or long engine with upgraded cooling components
repairOptions.longevity: Only option after catastrophic overheating or fire. Ensure recall completed on replacement

problems.recommendation: If you own a 2017-2023 petrol Velar, verify the cooling system recall has been completed immediately. This is not optional. A preventative £900-£1,400 specialist cooling overhaul is cheap insurance against a potential engine bay fire. For Velars already suffering overheating damage, be honest about severity - minor gasket failures can be repaired, but any fire damage or seizure requires full replacement.
problems.cta: Get quotes for PT204 Velar petrol engine replacement and cooling system repairs

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Range Rover Velar
finalCta.paragraph: The Ingenium diesel timing chain and oil dilution failures are well-documented, but they don't have to total your Velar. With a Velar retaining £15,000-£25,000 of value, an £8,500-£9,500 specialist replacement engine makes financial sense compared to buying another used Velar with the same underlying weaknesses. EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists who supply reconditioned engines fitted with upgraded timing components, revised piston rings, and the latest ECU calibrations - delivered nationwide with a 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare prices from our vetted network and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Range Rover Velar Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Range Rover Velar and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

types.title: Range Rover Velar Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Factory-spec dismantle, inspection, machining and reassembly with new parts where tolerances exceed wear limits.
types.frontDescription: Factory-spec dismantle, inspection, machining and reassembly with new parts where tolerances exceed wear limits.
types.backDescription: Reconditioned engines are stripped to bare block, ultrasonically cleaned, crack tested and machined to factory tolerances with new piston rings, bearings and seals.
backBullets: Ideal for Velar owners wanting maximum reliability after Ingenium timing chain failure
204DTA and 204DTD units rebuilt with upgraded timing components
12 month unlimited mileage warranty, typical turnaround 5-10 working days
types.cta: Compare reconditioned Range Rover Velar engine prices

types.title: Range Rover Velar Rebuilt Engines
types.description: Stripped and reassembled with new gaskets, seals, piston rings and bearings - retains some original wear components.
types.frontDescription: Stripped and reassembled with new gaskets, seals, piston rings and bearings - retains some original wear components.
types.frontDisclaimer: Mid-range option, good balance of cost and quality.
types.backDescription: Rebuilt engines focus on wear-related replacement without full remanufacturing standards - cost-effective for Velars with moderate damage but sound core blocks.
backBullets: New timing chains, tensioners and oil pumps fitted to address common failure points
Shorter lead time than full reconditioning, typically 3-7 working days
Suitable for D180, D240, P250 and P300 Velar variants
types.cta: Get rebuilt Range Rover Velar engine quotes

types.title: Range Rover Velar Used Engines
types.description: Low-mileage salvage units with documented history, tested for compression and oil pressure before supply.
types.frontDescription: Low-mileage salvage units with documented history, tested for compression and oil pressure before supply.
types.backDescription: Used engines offer the lowest upfront cost but carry the same original design weaknesses that caused failure in your Velar - buyer diligence essential.
backBullets: Warranty typically 30-90 days only, unlike 12 months on reconditioned units
Same timing chain and oil dilution risks present on 204DTA and 204DTD salvage units
Best for low-mileage donors with documented service history and pre-2019 build dates
types.cta: Check used Range Rover Velar engine availability

types.title: Range Rover Velar Remanufactured Engines
types.description: Industrial-grade rebuild to exceed original OEM specifications with all wear components replaced, not inspected.
types.frontDescription: Industrial-grade rebuild to exceed original OEM specifications with all wear components replaced, not inspected.
types.backDescription: Remanufacturing goes beyond reconditioning - every moving part is replaced including crankshaft regrind, cylinder bore honing and oversized pistons fitted.
backBullets: Engine code specific calibration for 204DTA, 204DTD, AJ126 and PT204 units
Upgraded timing chain tensioners and revised injector seals as standard
Extended warranty options up to 24 months unlimited mileage available
types.cta: Compare remanufactured Range Rover Velar engine costs

types.title: Range Rover Velar Refurbished Engines
types.description: Cleaned, inspected and tested with replacement of only failed or visibly worn components.
types.frontDescription: Cleaned, inspected and tested with replacement of only failed or visibly worn components.
types.backDescription: Refurbished engines sit between used and rebuilt - cosmetic and mechanical cleaning with component testing but no full dimensional remanufacturing.
backBullets: Faster turnaround than reconditioned, typically 2-5 working days
Lower cost but does not address all potential failure modes
Suitable for Velars with minor overheating or single-component failure only
types.cta: View refurbished Range Rover Velar engine options

types.title: Range Rover Velar Supply & Fit Engines
types.description: Fully fitted installation by UK specialist workshops including old unit removal, new engine fitting and ECU coding.

types.frontDisclaimer: Labour varies by engine code and workshop location.
types.backDescription: Supply and fit packages remove the complexity of finding a separate installer - specialists handle the entire job including ZF 8-speed gearbox alignment and Ingenium ECU programming.
backBullets: Includes coolant, oil, filters, gaskets and all replacement consumables
Mobile fitting available for many Velar variants across UK mainland

engineSizes.h2: Range Rover Velar Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Velar is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years, power outputs and common failure notes - helping you identify your exact unit before comparing replacement quotes.

groups.title: Range Rover Velar Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Range Rover Velar 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre Ingenium diesel formed the backbone of the Velar range from launch in 2017, powering over 80% of UK sales across D180, D200, D240 and D250 variants. Available in single-turbo (150-180hp) and twin-turbo (240-250hp) configurations, this JLR-designed unit prioritised efficiency but gained a reputation for timing chain and oil dilution failures that drive most Velar replacement enquiries today.
commonFailurePoints: oil dilution from incomplete DPF regeneration
turbo bearing failure on twin-turbo 204DTA units
items.cta: Get quotes for Range Rover Velar 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Range Rover Velar 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre Ford-sourced AJD V6 diesel arrived as the premium diesel option for Velar buyers wanting six-cylinder refinement without moving to petrol. Offered as D300 and later D350, this unit shares its architecture with the Discovery and Range Rover Sport - delivering 300-350hp but carrying the same crankshaft vulnerability that plagues the TDV6 family across JLR models.
items.cta: Get quotes for Range Rover Velar 3.0L diesel engines

groups.title: Range Rover Velar Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Range Rover Velar 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre Ingenium petrol launched alongside its diesel sibling, offering P250 and P300 variants with 250-300hp from the PT204 engine code. Generally more reliable than the diesel Ingenium, these units affect fewer replacement enquiries - but a major cooling system fire risk recall covering 2017-2023 models has made them a focus for preventative replacement and post-failure repairs.
commonFailurePoints: Cooling system component failure causing engine fire risk (recall issued)
coolant leak from plastic crossover pipes
timing chain tensioner wear (lower incidence than diesel)
items.cta: Compare Range Rover Velar 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Range Rover Velar 3.0L Supercharged Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre supercharged V6 brought genuine performance to the Velar range between 2017 and 2020, powering P340, P380 and early P400 variants before the Velar consolidated around four-cylinder Ingenium units. Derived from Ford's AJ126 architecture and shared with Jaguar F-Pace, these engines deliver supercharged character at the cost of specialist-only replacement complexity and higher parts prices.
commonFailurePoints: coolant leaks from plastic crossover pipes
items.cta: Compare Range Rover Velar 3.0L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Range Rover Velar Hybrid Engine Sizes

items.title: Range Rover Velar 2.0L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The P400e plug-in hybrid combined the PT204 2.0-litre petrol engine with a 105kW electric motor and 17.1kWh battery, producing a combined 404hp - the most powerful Velar until the V8 conversions. Available from 2019 onwards, these complex powertrains require specialist hybrid-trained technicians for engine replacement, with both combustion and electric drive units demanding careful ECU integration.
items.cta: Enquire about Range Rover Velar 2.0L hybrid engine availability

fuelTypes.h2: Range Rover Velar Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Range Rover Velar engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from the economical D180 diesel to the high-performance P400 supercharged petrol.

items.title: Range Rover Velar Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel-powered Velars dominate UK replacement enquiries, with the 2.0-litre Ingenium units (D180, D240, D250) accounting for over 80% of catastrophic timing chain and oil dilution failures.
items.descriptor: Diesel-powered Velars dominate UK replacement enquiries, with the 2.0-litre Ingenium units (D180, D240, D250) accounting for over 80% of catastrophic timing chain and oil dilution failures.
items.familiesLabel: Common Range Rover Velar Diesel Engine Families
families: 204DTD - single-turbo 2.0L Ingenium, fitted to D180 and D200 variants
204DTA - twin-turbo 2.0L Ingenium, fitted to D240 and D250 variants with higher failure rate
306DT - Ford-sourced 3.0L V6 (TDV6/SDV6), fitted to D300 and D350, rare but crankshaft-sensitive
foundIn: First Generation Velar (L560, 2017-2024) - all diesel variants across full production run
knownFor: Strong motorway economy (45-50mpg real-world on 2.0L units)
Timing chain stretch on 204DTD and 204DTA - #1 replacement driver
Oil dilution from incomplete DPF regeneration destroying bearings
Twin-turbo failure on D240/D250 sending metal through engine
D300/D350 V6 carries TDV6 crankshaft failure risk
items.cta: Get quotes for Range Rover Velar Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Range Rover Velar Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Velars generate lower replacement volume than diesel but higher enquiry value per unit, with the 2.0L Ingenium PT204 affected by a major cooling system fire recall and the supercharged V6 variants requiring specialist rebuild expertise.
items.descriptor: Petrol Velars generate lower replacement volume than diesel but higher enquiry value per unit, with the 2.0L Ingenium PT204 affected by a major cooling system fire recall and the supercharged V6 variants requiring specialist rebuild expertise.
items.familiesLabel: Common Range Rover Velar Petrol Engine Families
families: PT204 - 2.0L turbocharged Ingenium four-cylinder, fitted to P250 and P300 variants
AJ126 - 3.0L supercharged V6 (Ford-derived), fitted to P340 and P380 (2017-2020)
AJ133 - 5.0L supercharged V8, extremely rare aftermarket conversions only (not factory UK)
foundIn: First Generation Velar (L560, 2017-2024) - P250, P300 across full production; P340/P380 2017-2020 only
knownFor: Fewer catastrophic failures than diesel Ingenium units
Cooling system fire risk recall affecting all 2017-2023 PT204 petrol Velars
Supercharger bearing wear on AJ126 V6 units (60,000-90,000 miles)
Higher replacement complexity and parts cost than four-cylinder units
Petrol Velar owners less price-sensitive than diesel owners

items.title: Range Rover Velar Plug-in Hybrid (PHEV) Engines
items.description: The P400e plug-in hybrid combines the PT204 2.0L petrol engine with a 105kW electric motor and 17.1kWh battery - the most powerful factory Velar at 404hp, but requiring specialist hybrid-trained technicians for engine replacement.
items.descriptor: The P400e plug-in hybrid combines the PT204 2.0L petrol engine with a 105kW electric motor and 17.1kWh battery - the most powerful factory Velar at 404hp, but requiring specialist hybrid-trained technicians for engine replacement.
items.familiesLabel: Range Rover Velar PHEV Engine Codes
families: PT204 - 2.0L turbocharged Ingenium petrol (296hp combustion component)
Electric drive unit - 105kW permanent magnet synchronous motor
31-mile electric-only range from 17.1kWh battery under boot floor
Petrol engine automatically engages for longer journeys or when battery depletes
ZF 8-speed automatic with integrated electric motor replaces conventional torque converter
foundIn: First Generation Velar (L560, 2019-2024) - P400e and P400e R-Dynamic trims
importantNotes: Engine replacement requires high-voltage system isolation and hybrid component inspection
Battery health check strongly recommended during any combustion engine replacement
Fewer specialists qualified for PHEV work than standard petrol/diesel Velars
Cooling system fire risk recall applies to PT204 component of P400e

engineYears.h2: Range Rover Velar Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: The Velar launched in 2017 on JLR's D7a aluminium platform and received major engine updates in 2018, 2020, and 2024 - the table below maps every UK-market engine to its production period so you can identify your exact unit and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.description: The Velar launches with the full Ingenium four-cylinder range - the diesel timing chain failures that would dominate replacement enquiries begin appearing within 50,000 miles.
keyChanges: Velar name revived from 1969 prototype codename - positioned between Evoque and Range Rover Sport
D7a aluminium-intensive platform shared with Jaguar F-Pace
2.0L Ingenium diesel four-cylinder (D180 single-turbo, D240 twin-turbo) and petrol (P250, P300)
ZF 8HP45 transmission on four-cylinder models, 8HP70 on six-cylinder models
Touch Pro Duo twin-screen infotainment system debuts
popularModels: Range Rover Velar D180 · Range Rover Velar D240 · Range Rover Velar P250
Range Rover Velar P300 · Range Rover Velar D300 · Range Rover Velar P380
knownFor: Most UK replacement enquiries centre on 2017-2018 Ingenium diesel units
Timing chain stretch documented as early as 40,000 miles on early production
Oil dilution from DPF regen cycles - rising oil level destroys bottom end bearings
P380 supercharged V6 carries supercharger coupler failure risk (60,000-90,000 miles)

years.preview: 2018 petrol models affected by cooling system fire risk recall
years.cta: Get quotes for 2017-2018 Range Rover Velar replacement engines
years.ticker: Range Rover Velar D240 engine replacement • 204DTA timing chain failure cost • Velar D180 seized engine • Range Rover Velar 2018 oil dilution repair
enquiries: Range Rover Velar D240 engine replacement
Range Rover Velar 2018 oil dilution repair

years.description: JLR expands the V6 offerings with a lower-output diesel D275 and P340 supercharged petrol, while the short-lived SVAutobiography Dynamic V8 arrives.
keyChanges: D275 3.0L V6 diesel arrives (275hp) as mid-range option
P340 3.0L supercharged V6 petrol joins the range (340hp)
SVAutobiography Dynamic Edition launched with 5.0L supercharged V8
Apple CarPlay and Android Auto become standard
Adaptive damping suspension now standard across range
popularModels: Range Rover Velar D275 · Range Rover Velar P340
knownFor: First Edition discontinued - replaced by SVAutobiography Dynamic as range-topper
D275 bridges gap between four-cylinder economy and D300 performance
Supercharged V8 extremely rare - low search volume but very high replacement cost
V6 petrol units carry supercharger bearing wear as primary failure mode
2019 models see first reliability improvements to timing chain tensioners
years.cta: Compare 2018-2019 Range Rover Velar V6 and V8 engine prices

years.description: The Ingenium diesel range is restructured - D180 becomes D200, D240 becomes D250 - and the 3.0L V6 diesel gains an extra 35hp as D350 joins.
keyChanges: D180 renamed D200 with power bump to 204hp (same 204DTD block)
D240 renamed D250 with power increase to 250hp (same 204DTA architecture)
New 3.0L V6 diesel D350 joins range (350hp from 306DT)
V6 petrol models now use turbocharged inline-six with mild-hybrid tech
Standard air suspension fitted to all V6 models
Larger fuel tank on six-cylinder variants
mainEngines: AJ20P6 - 3.0L turbocharged I6 petrol (P400, 400hp with 48V MHEV)
popularModels: Range Rover Velar D200 · Range Rover Velar D250 · Range Rover Velar D350
knownFor: D200/D250 retain same timing chain and oil dilution weaknesses as earlier units
D350 offers genuine performance diesel option - 350hp, 700Nm torque
P400 mild-hybrid uses belt-integrated starter-generator (BISG) and 48V battery
2020 models see some timing chain tensioner revisions but failures still reported
V6 petrol units now turbocharged rather than supercharged - different character
years.cta: Get quotes for 2020 Range Rover Velar diesel and MHEV replacement engines

years.description: The P400e plug-in hybrid arrives as the most powerful Velar at 404hp, while the supercharged V6 and V8 petrols are phased out.
keyChanges: P400e plug-in hybrid launches - PT204 petrol engine + 105kW electric motor
17.1kWh battery provides 31-mile electric-only range
Supercharged V6 (AJ126) and V8 (AJ133) discontinued after 2020
Pivi Pro infotainment replaces Touch Pro Duo
Cabin air purification system (Purify Cycle) introduced
mainEngines: PT204 PHEV - 2.0L petrol + electric motor (P400e, 404hp combined)
popularModels: Range Rover Velar P400e · Range Rover Velar D350
knownFor: P400e most complex Velar powertrain - requires hybrid-specialist technicians
PHEV battery cooling system failures documented in early production
Supercharged V6 and V8 become sought-after performance units post-discontinuation
Diesel Ingenium 204DTA/204DTD still carry timing chain weakness
2021 models get improved Pivi Pro system - fewer screen failures
years.cta: Compare Range Rover Velar P400e hybrid and diesel replacement prices

years.description: One-year HST trim with 395hp mild-hybrid six-cylinder arrives, followed by 2024 facelift with refreshed styling and upgraded Pivi Pro.
keyChanges: HST trim offered for one year (2023) - P400 MHEV engine, black 21-inch wheels
2024 model receives exterior refresh and redesigned centre console
Next-generation Pivi Pro with 11.4-inch floating touchscreen
Carbon dioxide monitoring with Cabin Air Purification Plus
P250 models reportedly receive smoother transmission shifting
popularModels: Range Rover Velar HST (2023 only) · Range Rover Velar Dynamic SE
knownFor: 2023 HST essentially a run-out special before 2024 facelift
Late-model Ingenium diesels still carry oil dilution risk - no fundamental redesign
2024 P250 transmission mapping improved for smoother shifts
Cooling system fire risk recall still applies to 2017-2023 PT204 petrol units
HST trim offers P400 performance with unique visual package

faq.h2: Range Rover Velar Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Range Rover Velar engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A full Range Rover Velar engine replacement typically costs £8,500-£13,000 supply and fit, depending on engine type and condition. Specialist independent garages charge significantly less than main dealers.
keyPoints: Rebuilt 2.0L Ingenium diesel (D180/D240): £8,592-£9,480 fitted
Used engine (supply only): £3,500-£6,500 + £600-£1,200 fitting
Main dealer replacement: £12,000-£16,000 (typically £3,000+ more than specialist)
P400e hybrid replacement: £9,000-£14,000 due to hybrid system complexity
3.0L V6 diesel (D300/D350): £7,800-£12,500 supply only
items.cta: Compare live Range Rover Velar engine replacement quotes from UK specialists

items.question: Are Range Rover Velar engines reliable? Which engine should I avoid?
items.answer: The 2.0L Ingenium diesel (D180/D240) has documented timing chain and oil dilution failures. The 3.0L petrol V6 and newer Ingenium inline-six are more reliable choices.
keyPoints: 2.0L Ingenium diesel (2017-2020): highest failure rate - timing chain stretch 50k-80k miles
2.0L Ingenium petrol (PT204): fewer failures but cooling system fire risk recall
3.0L V6 diesel (D300/D350): rare but carries TDV6 crankshaft failure risk
3.0L Ingenium inline-six (2020+): strongest modern Velar engine reliability record
Supercharged 5.0L V8 (SVAutobiography): very rare, high maintenance costs
items.cta: Find a reconditioned Range Rover Velar engine with upgraded timing components

items.question: What causes the Range Rover Velar 2.0 Ingenium diesel engine to fail?
items.answer: Timing chain stretch and oil dilution from incomplete DPF regeneration are the two primary failure modes. Both lead to catastrophic bearing failure and complete engine seizure.
keyPoints: Timing chain tensioner design fails - chain stretches, skips teeth, pistons hit valves
Oil dilution - diesel fuel enters oil during failed DPF regens, destroying bearings
Twin-turbo D240 units suffer additional turbo bearing failure sending metal through engine
Bottom-end knock typically starts without warning lights - check oil level weekly
Full engine replacement required once bearing failure occurs - repair not viable
items.cta: Get quotes for 204DTA or 204DTD Velar engine replacement

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Range Rover Velar?
items.answer: Yes. With a well-maintained Velar worth £15,000-£25,000, an £8,500-£9,500 specialist replacement engine is far more cost-effective than buying another used Velar with the same underlying risks.
keyPoints: Velar resale values: 2017-2019 models £15k-£20k, 2020+ models £20k-£30k
Replacement with upgraded components addresses original design flaws
Used Velar of same age carries identical timing chain/oil dilution risks
Specialist replacement (£8.5k-£9.5k fitted) vs dealer (£12k-£16k) saves thousands
Reconditioned engines include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty as standard
items.cta: Compare Range Rover Velar rebuilt engine prices vs new car alternatives

items.question: How long does a Range Rover Velar engine replacement take?
items.answer: Most Range Rover Velar engine replacements take 5-10 working days from booking to completion, including delivery of the replacement unit and professional fitting by a specialist.
keyPoints: Standard lead time: 3-5 days for engine delivery + 2-5 days for fitting
Complex variants (P400e, D300 V6): 7-14 working days
Mobile fitting available for 2.0L Ingenium units across mainland UK
ECU coding and ZF gearbox alignment included in specialist fitting packages
Courtesy car available from many suppliers during replacement work
items.cta: Get time estimates and quotes for Range Rover Velar engine fitting

items.question: What warranty comes with a replacement Range Rover Velar engine?
items.answer: All reconditioned Velar engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Extended 24-month warranties are available on premium rebuilt units.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - standard on all reconditioned units
24-month warranty available on premium rebuilds (typically £400-£600 extra)
Used engines: 30-90 day warranty only - buyer beware on Ingenium units
Warranty covers manufacturing defects and premature component failure
Parts-only vs supply & fit warranty - always confirm coverage terms
items.cta: Compare Range Rover Velar engine warranty options from UK specialists

items.question: Will a used Range Rover Velar engine have the same problems?
items.answer: Yes - used 2.0L Ingenium diesel engines from 2017-2020 have identical timing chain and oil dilution weaknesses. Only buy used if the seller provides documented evidence of upgraded timing components.
keyPoints: Used engines carry original design flaws - no manufacturing improvements
Timing chain tensioner failure risk remains on pre-2020 units
Oil dilution damage may already be present but invisible
2021+ late-production units have revised tensioners but failures still reported
Reconditioned units always include upgraded chain components - used units don't
items.cta: Enquire about reconditioned Velar engines with upgraded timing chains

items.question: Can I fit a Range Rover Velar engine myself?
items.answer: Not recommended. Velar engine replacement requires specialist knowledge of the ZF 8-speed gearbox alignment, Ingenium ECU coding, and JLR D7a platform procedures that DIY mechanics cannot complete.
keyPoints: ZF 8HP transmission alignment requires diagnostic software DIY owners lack
ECU must be reprogrammed to match new engine - immobiliser issues common
AdBlue and DPF systems need recalibration after diesel engine swaps
Engine hoist and engine support beam required - Velar engine bay is cramped
No warranty on DIY supply-only engines - fitting errors not covered
items.cta: Find specialist Range Rover Velar engine supply and fit deals

items.question: How do I know if my Velar has the 204DTA or 204DTD engine?
items.answer: Check your V5C logbook or the engine code sticker under the bonnet. D180/D200 models use 204DTD single-turbo. D240/D250 models use 204DTA twin-turbo - the higher failure rate variant.
keyPoints: 204DTD: Single-turbo, 180-204hp, fitted to D180 and D200 models
204DTA: Twin-turbo, 240-250hp, fitted to D240 and D250 models
Engine code stamped on cylinder block near oil filter housing
V5C logbook section 4 shows variant (D180/D240 etc.) to confirm
Enter your reg number above for instant engine code identification
items.cta: Get your Range Rover Velar engine code identified and compare quotes

items.question: Does the Range Rover Velar P400e hybrid engine have different replacement considerations?
items.answer: Yes - P400e replacement costs £9,000-£14,000 and requires hybrid-trained specialists. The electric motor, battery and cooling system must be inspected and isolated before combustion engine work.
keyPoints: High-voltage system isolation required - not all garages qualified
Battery health check strongly recommended during engine replacement
PT204 combustion component carries same cooling system recall as standard petrol
17.1kWh battery replacement (if needed) adds £5,000-£8,000 to costs
Fewer specialists available - expect longer lead times
items.cta: Enquire about Range Rover Velar P400e hybrid engine replacement specialists
